desc: Florence is a compact Renaissance city where masterpieces by Michelangelo, Botticelli, and Brunelleschi are part of the daily streetscape. The historic core is walkable, intensely cultural, and ideal for travelers who want museum-heavy days balanced by Tuscan food, river views, and quick rail access to nearby towns.
hls: Renaissance icons: Duomo, Uffizi, Accademia, Ponte Vecchio
Dense, walkable center with major sights close together
Top-tier art collections and church fresco cycles
Excellent Tuscan cuisine and wine bars in Oltrarno
Panoramic viewpoints from Piazzale Michelangelo and San Miniato
Easy day trips to Siena, Pisa, Lucca, and Chianti

todo.name: Duomo complex (Cathedral, Dome, Baptistery)
todo.desc: Book combined tickets to climb Brunelleschi's dome and visit the cathedral museum.

todo.name: Uffizi Galleries
todo.desc: See Botticelli, Leonardo, and Caravaggio in one of Europe's essential collections.

todo.name: Accademia Gallery
todo.desc: Visit Michelangelo's David and unfinished Prisoners sculptures.

todo.name: Ponte Vecchio and Arno river walk
todo.desc: Cross Florence's iconic bridge and continue along both riverbanks at sunset.

todo.name: Palazzo Pitti & Boboli Gardens
todo.desc: Combine palace galleries with landscaped gardens behind the Oltrarno district.

todo.name: Mercato Centrale
todo.desc: Try Tuscan specialties and browse produce, cheeses, and regional deli stalls.

todo.name: Piazzale Michelangelo viewpoint
todo.desc: Walk or bus up for classic skyline views over the Duomo and river.

todo.name: Chianti or Siena day trip
todo.desc: Use Florence as a base for Tuscan vineyards, hill towns, and medieval centers.

wifi.note: Reliable in central stays and coworking spaces; older historic buildings can have weaker in-room signals.

prac.fltNote: Direct Gatwick flights to Florence are limited and seasonal; many travelers route via Pisa, Bologna, Milan, or Rome and continue by train.
prac.visa: Italy is in the Schengen Area; many non-EU visitors (e.g., US/UK) can stay up to 90 days in any 180-day period visa-free.

sweet: Late March to May and October are strongest for museum visits and walking: pleasant temperatures, manageable lines, and better hotel value than summer.
